title: Consumer Protection
summary: Consumer Protection means safeguarding the interests of consumers from unfair trade practices, defective goods, and deficient services.
Consumer Protection Act 2019 replaced the old 1986 Act. Defines consumer, rights, and redressal machinery.
Consumer = a person who buys goods/services for personal use (not for resale or commercial purpose).
6 Consumer Rights: Right to Safety, Right to be Informed, Right to Choose, Right to be Heard, Right to Seek Redressal, Right to Consumer Education.
Redressal machinery (3-tier): District Commission (up to ₹1 crore) → State Commission (₹1-10 crore) → National Commission (above ₹10 crore).
Consumer responsibilities: Be aware, buy quality-marked products (ISI, AGMARK, FPO), ask for receipts, file complaints when wronged.
Role of consumer organisations and NGOs in spreading awareness — 'JAGO GRAHAK JAGO' campaign.

teacher_tip.title: AKSpected Shortcut
teacher_tip.text: If the question says 'goods worth ₹50 lakh' → District Commission. '₹5 crore' → State Commission. '₹15 crore' → National Commission. Remember quality marks: ISI = industrial products, AGMARK = agricultural products, FPO = processed food, Hallmark = gold/jewellery. 'JAGO GRAHAK JAGO' = government consumer awareness campaign.

sections.title: Meaning of Consumer Protection

definition.text: Safeguarding the interests and rights of consumers against unethical and unfair trade practices by sellers.

sections.content: Consumer protection is needed because consumers are often exploited through adulteration, false advertising, defective products, unfair pricing, and deficient services.

sections.title: Consumer Protection Act 2019
points: Replaced the Consumer Protection Act, 1986.
Consumer = any person who buys goods or hires services for consideration. Does NOT include: a person who buys for resale or commercial purpose.
Complaint can be filed by: Consumer, Any recognised consumer association, Central/State Government, Legal representative (if consumer is deceased).
Grounds for complaint: Defective goods, Deficient services, Overcharging, Misleading advertisements, Offering hazardous goods.

sections.title: Consumer Rights
points: 1. Right to Safety: Protection against goods hazardous to life and property. Example: ISI mark on electrical appliances.
2. Right to be Informed: Right to have complete information about quality, quantity, price, ingredients. Example: MRP, manufacturing/expiry date on labels.
3. Right to Choose: Freedom to select from a variety of goods and services at competitive prices.
4. Right to be Heard: Consumer's interests will receive due consideration in forums and policies.
5. Right to Seek Redressal: Right to fair settlement of genuine grievances — replacement, removal of defect, compensation.
6. Right to Consumer Education: Right to know about consumer rights and responsibilities.

sections.title: Redressal Machinery (3-Tier)
points: 1. District Commission: Handles complaints up to ₹1 crore. Headed by President (District Judge level).
2. State Commission: Handles complaints from ₹1 crore to ₹10 crore. Also hears appeals against District Commission orders.
3. National Commission: Handles complaints above ₹10 crore. Also hears appeals against State Commission orders. Located in New Delhi.
Appeals: District → State → National → Supreme Court.

sections.title: Consumer Responsibilities
points: Be aware of rights and remedies available.
Buy only standardised goods — look for ISI, AGMARK, FPO, Hallmark.
Ask for cash memo/receipt/bill for every purchase.
File complaints against genuine grievances — don't remain silent.
Know the product details — read labels carefully.
Be an ethical consumer — don't buy smuggled or counterfeit goods.

sections.title: Role of Consumer Organisations & NGOs
points: Educate consumers about their rights and responsibilities.
Publish journals and magazines spreading awareness.
File complaints on behalf of consumers.
Encourage standardisation (ISI, AGMARK).
Government campaign: 'JAGO GRAHAK JAGO' (Wake Up Consumer) — awareness through TV, radio, and print.

tables.title: Three-Tier Redressal Machinery
tables.tip: Know the monetary limits — frequently asked as MCQ.

tables.title: Quality Certification Marks
tables.tip: Know at least 4 marks for MCQ.

questions.text: A complaint for goods worth ₹8 crore should be filed with:
questions.answer: State Commission — handles complaints from ₹1 crore to ₹10 crore.

questions.text: Which quality mark is used for agricultural products?
questions.answer: AGMARK — used for agricultural products like spices, honey, and ghee.

(i) Right to Safety: Protection against hazardous goods. Consumers should buy ISI-marked products.
(ii) Right to be Informed: Right to full information about quality, quantity, price, and ingredients on the product label.
(iii) Right to Seek Redressal: Right to fair settlement of grievances through consumer courts — replacement, repair, or compensation.

questions.text:
Ramesh bought an electric heater worth ₹3,000 with ISI mark. Within a week, it caused a short circuit and damaged his furniture. He wants to file a complaint.

Identify which consumer right is violated and where should Ramesh file the complaint.
questions.answer:
1. Right to Safety violated: Despite having ISI mark, the product was hazardous and caused damage to property.
2. District Commission: Since the value of goods + compensation claim would be under ₹1 crore, the complaint should be filed with the District Commission.
3. Remedies available: Replacement of defective heater, compensation for damaged furniture, and removal of defect.

pyqs.text: Explain the three-tier redressal machinery under the Consumer Protection Act 2019.
pyqs.answer:
(i) District Commission: Handles complaints up to ₹1 crore.
(ii) State Commission: Handles ₹1-10 crore and appeals from District Commission.
(iii) National Commission: Handles above ₹10 crore and appeals from State Commission. Located in New Delhi.

related_chapters.title: Marketing
related_chapters.reason: Consumer protection safeguards against unfair marketing practices

related_chapters.title: Business Environment
related_chapters.reason: Legal environment includes consumer protection laws

mnemonics.title: Redressal Limits — 1-10-Above
mnemonics.text: District: up to ₹1 Cr · State: ₹1-10 Cr · National: Above ₹10 Cr
